text: Artificial intelligence is becoming more common in our daily lives. However, its growth brings important ethical challenges. One major issue is bias, which can occur when AI systems learn from unfair or incomplete data, leading to unfair results for certain groups. Fairness means that these systems should treat everyone equally, avoiding discrimination. Privacy is another concern, as AI often relies on large amounts of personal data, raising questions about how that information is collected and protected. Transparency requires that people understand how AI makes decisions, which builds trust. For these reasons, responsible AI development is essential—developers and companies must work to ensure that AI serves society in just and beneficial ways.
